author: wiki-mod/distcc-ng maintainers
tooling: Hand-authored from GitHub Code Scanning's dismissed-alert history (repos/wiki-mod/distcc-ng/code-scanning/alerts?state=dismissed), not machine-generated by a scanner.

statements.status_notes: bulk.c:300 TOCTOU: received-file write is pre-created with O_EXCL 0600 in a sticky tmp (client) or confined to a mkdtemp() 0700 per-connection dir (server) -- no exploitable race window. Documented in #143, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.
tempfile.c:111 dcc_mk_tmpdir: all real call sites root at stable system dirs or 0700 mkdtemp() trees, no exploitable TOCTOU window. Documented in #143, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.

Pre-existing finding, unrelated to PR #175's diff (arg.c/serve.c only). Tracked for real triage/fix under issue #143; not fixed or dismissed as false-positive here, just re-filed out of this PR's CodeQL gate.
statements.action_statement: Tracked for final triage under issue #143 (not yet a closed won't-fix or false-positive determination); dismissed from the originating pull request's CodeQL gate to unblock that PR's merge, pending the real accept/fix decision.

statements.status_notes: Client-only SSH transport, hardened in PR #252 (dcc_ssh_cmd_is_sane). execvp's $PATH search is atomic, no TOCTOU. See #143 Group H, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.
hosts.c:610: DISTCC_HOSTS/config is local input, no wire caller reaches this in the daemon. Self-DoS at worst, no privilege boundary crossed. Documented in #143, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.
util.c:559 dcc_dup_part(): sole caller is hosts.c, same local host-list input as alert #13. Documented in #143, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.

Received compile output must match a real local compile's mode (0666) exactly, since distcc is a drop-in compiler replacement. test/testdistcc.py's ModeBits_Case asserts this. Tightening it broke that test in real CI (PR #158).
Deliberate: 0666 lock file intentionally shared across multiple local users on one DISTCC_DIR. Already documented in src/lock.c's own comment. Confirmed via PR #158 review, 2026-07-17.

Deliberate design: DISTCC_LOG mode 0666 matches every other distcc trace/lock/state file for 25+ yrs (see traceenv.c comment, lock.c dcc_open_lockfile()). Maintainer-confirmed, not an oversight. Verified via PR #152's real 2-host testing (2026-07-17).

statements.status_notes: compile.c:1133, mitigated by dcc_sane_env_path() at compile.c:837. Source is INCLUDE_SERVER_PORT, client-local env, not wire-supplied. Structural false positive per #193's sanitizer-recognition gap. Documented in #143, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.

vulnerability.name: distcc-ng-codeql-34
vulnerability.description: Uncontrolled data used in path expression

statements.status_notes: serve.c:272, DISTCC_CMDLIST is daemon-local env, gated by dcc_sane_env_path(). Extra hardening considered, not worth it (see #251): whoever sets it already has local machine control. Maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.

vulnerability.name: distcc-ng-codeql-36
vulnerability.description: Uncontrolled data used in path expression

statements.status_notes: dcc_sane_env_path() (src/pathsafety.c) genuinely sanitizes this before open(). CodeQL doesn't model custom sanitizers. A/B-verified: unpatched wrote a file with a raw control byte, patched never does. PR #152, 2026-07-17.

vulnerability.name: distcc-ng-codeql-38
vulnerability.description: Uncontrolled data used in path expression

statements.status_notes: Fixed in #93/#94: dcc_name_has_path_traversal() rejects unsafe NAME before this call. Verified via real e2e. Default cpp/path-injection query doesn't model a cross-file boolean validator as a barrier -- query limitation, not an open gap. link_target residual tracked in #95.

statements.status_notes: prefork.c:155: fopen("/proc/self/oom_score_adj","w") -- pre-existing kernel pseudo-file, O_CREAT/0666 semantics are no-ops on it, no world-writable file is ever actually created. opt_oom_score_adj is local daemon config. Documented in #143, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.

statements.status_notes: Intentional: same accepted design as alert #25 (lock.c:253) -- shared DISTCC_DIR lock dir needs 0666 so other local users can relock slot files. This fchmod is issue #159's actual fix (umask was masking the mode). Maintainer sign-off 2026-07-16.

traceenv.c:80: deliberate 0666 matching the long-standing shared-DISTCC_DIR convention also used by lock.c/bulk.c. Previously reviewed and accepted by the maintainer. Documented in #143/#157, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.

statements.status_notes: traceenv.c:80, mitigated by dcc_sane_env_path() at traceenv.c:66, client-only. Same call-site class as previously-dismissed #27/#36. Documented in #143, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.

New in PR #175 (client-side execlp of the user's own local compiler, not a remote trust-boundary crossing). Needs deeper investigation before a final decision - tracked under #143. Dismissed only to unblock this PR's merge.

statements.status_notes: lsdistcc.c:964 format string is fully constrained at runtime by dcc_thename_format_is_safe() (#226), which exit(1)s on any format not exactly one %d. Input is lsdistcc CLI arg, not wire-supplied. Structural false positive. Documented in #143, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.
Same code site and mitigation as alert #80 (lsdistcc.c:964, dcc_thename_format_is_safe()). Documented in #143, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.
Client-only (distcc_obj), hardened in PR #245 (absolute-path + access() recheck). Static model doesn't recognize runtime access() as a sanitizer for execl. See #254, maintainer sign-off 2026-07-19.
